label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what conclusion we can draw from the author's parents' story. The options are A through D. First, I need to recall the story. The author's parents had a kidney transplant where the mom donated a kidney to the dad. Before this, they had many quarrels. After the surgery, they showed more love and understanding for each other. The dad became gentler, the mom less angry. They developed a closer relationship and deepened their faith. The author also gave them photos of their hands on their wedding day and during recovery to symbolize their commitment. Looking at the options: A. Illness is part of daily life. The story mentions illness (disease leading to transplant), but it's not really the main point. The focus is more on the effect of the illness and the surgery on the relationship. B. Quarrels can't be avoided between parents. The story says the parents had years of quarrels before, but after the transplant, they changed. So the conclusion isn't that quarrels are unavoidable, but that they can be overcome. C. The relationship between parents affects children greatly. The author does mention that witnessing their parents' reconciliation and love had an impact, but the main focus is on the change in the parents' relationship due to the transplant. D. Suffering can better people's relationship as well. The parents went through suffering (illness, surgery) which led to a better relationship. The story emphasizes that the transplant healed the family, leading to a deeper bond
